Event title: Live reggae from Roots Alley Collective featuring special guest Dion Knibb
description: In celebration of Juneteenth and presented in collaboration with MIT Sloan School of Management and MIT’s African, Black, American, Caribbean Employee Resource Group, enjoy live music plus delicious food truck options, lawn games and more.

Event title: Park Sounds: Juneteenth Celebration with Tim Hall
description: PARK SOUNDS 2026 brings free outdoor musical performances featuring local artists to the front lawn of the Cambridge Public Library Main Branch. Tim Hall is an award-winning musician, performance poet and producer from Detroit who performs warm neo soul, jazz, and hip hop influenced music. The events are all-ages. Bring a chair, food and loved ones to celebrate the summer.

Event title: Soccer Watch Party: Scotland vs. Morocco
Location: Inman Square, Cambridge St. between Springfield and Prospect streets.
description: Enjoy free food, face painting, soccer activities and live entertainment. This event is part of “Cambridge United: Where the World Comes Together,” a citywide series of free, outdoor soccer screenings and community celebrations throughout summer 2026.

Event title: Jack Grady performs
description: Jack Grady is a comedy magician whose chaotic, unpredictable style has earned him a cult following and millions of fans across the internet.

Event title: ¡Música! with Mariana Iranzi
description: A bilingual family concert in English and Spanish. Recommended for children of all ages and their caregivers.

Event title: Saturday Salsa night: La Diáspora Combo
description: Led by lead vocalist and multi-instrumentalist Angel Javier Camilo, La Diáspora Combo is an intergenerational collective of musicians from Latin America and the United States. Rooted in diasporic identity and community, the ensemble brings high-energy, dance-forward performances of salsa, merengue, and bachata.

Event title: Mount Auburn Summer Solstice 2026
description: Offerings of free and paid activities including mindfulness walks, yoga, a cyanotype making workshop, meditation, a poetry reading and community concert.

Event title: Summer Solstice Celebration 2026: Night at the Harvard Museums of Science & Culture
description: Enjoy live music and performers, play lawn games and make a flower crown. Ice cream, beverages and food will be available for purchase from food trucks.

Event title: Cambridge Cooks: Caribbean Heritage Month Edition with Chef Gaitskell
description: Join Chef Gaitskell Cleghorn Jr. as he prepares jerk chicken and mango salsa in celebration of Caribbean-American Heritage Month.

Event title: Bluegrass: Sam Reid & The Riot Act
description: Sam Reid and the Riot Act is an American roots and bluegrass band that is acoustic guitar driven.

Event title: Soccer Watch Party: England vs. Ghana
description: Enjoy free food, face painting, soccer activities and live entertainment. This event is part of “Cambridge United: Where the World Comes Together,” a citywide series of free, outdoor soccer screenings and community celebrations throughout summer 2026.

Event title: Berklee Summer in the City: The Apparatus Trio
Venue: Urban Park Roof Garden at Kendall Center
description: The Apparatus Trio is a jazz fusion group redefining the edges of groove, improvisation and modern sonic textures.

Event title: Lunchtime concert: Rosa Joe Jacobs
Venue: Urban Park Roof Garden at Kendall Center
description: Rosa Joe Jacobs is a Brooklyn-bred, now Boston-based songwriter and musician. Her songs are drawn from folk traditions. Her debut is set to come out in 2027.

Event title: Astonished By Beauty: The Essential Practice of Natural History
description: Join naturalist Thomas Fleischner on his new book, “Astonished by Beauty,” a guide to reconnecting with nature through natural history.

Event title: Harvard Art Museums at Night
description: Gather with friends and mingle inside an Italian-inspired courtyard while taking in the smooth sounds from DJ C-Zone. Stop by the Materials Lab for a fun art-making activity and then drop in on one of the museum’s Spotlight Tours.

Event title: Cookbook Club and Community Potluck
Venue: Cambridge Public Library’s Central Square Branch
description: This month, we’re cooking from “In Bibi’s Kitchen: The Recipes and Stories of Grandmothers from the Eight African Countries that Touch the Indian Ocean” by Hawa Hassan. Bring a dish to share or just come to talk about your experience with this cookbook.

Event title: Gallery Performance: “Celtic Art Across the Ages”
description: This musical performance is related to the special exhibition, “Celtic Art Across the Ages.” Clare Fraser (fiddle) and Simon Lace (guitar/bouzouki) play traditional Irish music and talk about Celtic musical heritage.

Event title: 2026 Cambridge Dance Party
description: At 4 p.m., the Dance Party kicks off at City Hall with a high energy jam session and conga line ready set led by local favorite DJ P.A.C. that is specifically designed for older adults and young families. Then at 6 p.m., internationally-known DJ Gee Spin, a Boston-born radio legend whose career is deeply woven into the fabric of New England’s music scene is up and then, DJ D-Nice, the globally celebrated creator of Club Quarantine who began his career with the hip hop group Boogie Down Productions, closes out the night from 8 to 10 p.m.

Event title: The Femmes Pride Party
description: Join THE FEMMES, Boston’s all women and non-binary party band, for their annual Pride Party! Brand new songs that you’ve never heard at any other Femmes show, full horn section, three lead singers, and they’ll be playing all of your favorite and LGBTQ+ artists from 8pm-11pm. Get ready for a nonstop dance party and the night your life!

Event title: Family Fun Day
description: Celebrate the start of summer at the City’s 20th Annual Family Fun Day, hosted by the Parks & Rec Department. This year’s activities include bounce houses, magic shows, face painting, live music, arts and crafts, outdoor games and more.

Event title: Maggie O’Farrell reads from “Land”
description: The author of “Hamnet” and “The Marriage Portrait” discusses her new historical novel set in Ireland in the years before and after the Great Hunger. New York Times bestselling author Celeste Ng joins.

Event title: The Midnight Hour with Camila Quintero
description: Live music is back on stage at The Mad Monkfish! For those who can't make it to the Jazz Baroness Room, Monkfish now streams live all of its performances on their Facebook page. Not ready for the night to end? Welcome to Midnight at the Mad Monkfish with Camila Quintero!Camila Quintero, vocalsCamila Quintero, a 19-year-old undergraduate student at Berklee College of Music and graduate of Osceola County School for the Arts in Orlando Florida has pursued music since middle school. The Venezuelan vocalist captures audiences with her sensitive yet confident approach to singing styles such as jazz, Latin and contemporary music. Some of her achievements include being the vocalist for the Essentially Ellington jazz band competition winning ensemble for 2 consecutive years, receiving 2 Youngarts awards for jazz voice, and receiving two student Downbeat Jazz Magazine awards for outstanding jazz vocal performance and overall pop-blues vocal performance winner. She has been invited to perform at prestigious jazz venues such as “Judson’s Live” at Dr. Philip’s Center in Orlando, as well as the Florida State Capitol, the county City Hall, and government buildings invited by congressmen such as Darren Soto, Maxwell Frost and Olga Gonzalez for cultural Latin events, inaugurations and rallies amongst many other accomplishments in other fields of music. Additionally, she is expanding her education at Berklee College of Music where she forms part of challenging ensembles under the direction of Tia Fuller, Bijon Watson and others.

Event title: QA&P Presents: 48-Hour Play Festival
description: Across a single weekend, LGBTQ+ artists dream up, write down and fully conjure a collection of original one-act plays then perform them live on stage.

Event title: “Mare’s Nest”
description: In British filmmaker Ben Rivers’ new feature, told in a series of vignettes, a girl named Moon wanders through unfamiliar landscapes in a world seemingly free of adults. On her journey, Moon meets and observes many other children, learning from them about different ways of existing.

Event title: Dennis Brennan and The White Owls
description: This blues band features Dennis Brennan on vocals and harmonica, Tim Gearan on guitar, Steve Sadler on lap steel, Jim Haggerty on bass and Andy Plaisted on drums.

Event title: Somerville Fireworks
description: The celebration begins with live music from Plutos Return, a Somerville High School alumni band. DJ Live follows with a lively mix of pop, soul, funk and classic hits, and at 7:45 p.m., local funk band Search Party takes the stage. At 9 p.m., the National Anthem starts the countdown to fireworks. Rain date: July 1

Event title: Boston Moth StorySLAM: Holiday Gone Wrong
description: This community-focused, open-mic storytelling competition is open to anyone who can share a story on the night’s theme (changes every month).

Event title: Gallery Talk: American Works of Art at the 250th
description: Curator of American art Horace D. Ballard leads this series of gallery talks to coincide with the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. Each talk will closely examine a work of art that speaks to the historical, social and political contexts that continue to shape the events and ideas of the United States.

Event title: The Duppy Conquerors
description: This Bob Marley tribute band, the Duppy Conquerors, has been nominated for a Boston Music Award and includes a 10-piece ensemble of veteran musicians.

Event title: Portico Point to Pint Runners
description: Take a 3-mile run and have a beer afterwards. All paces are welcome and don’t forget your reflective gear and lights.
